findingDetails.title: The CSRF checklist labels a JavaScript-accessible session cookie as good guidance
findingDetails.evidence: The GOOD example creates a session cookie with document.cookie, which cannot set HttpOnly; lines 247-253 later prescribe server-side HttpOnly and call JavaScript-accessible session cookies bad.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The scope-corrected 157-Markdown search isolated this as the only positively labelled JavaScript session-cookie assignment; direct counterevidence in the same file confirms the server-side HttpOnly rule.
findingDetails.claim: The security checklist positively labels a JavaScript-set session cookie even though JavaScript cannot set HttpOnly and the same checklist later forbids JavaScript-accessible session cookies.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: The complete cookie guidance was reread; SameSite=Strict and Secure mitigate CSRF/transport risks, but the later HttpOnly rule confirms the JavaScript session-cookie example is incomplete and contradictory.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The first snippet may only demonstrate SameSite rather than complete session-cookie creation, but the GOOD label and session cookie name do not communicate that limitation.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the snippet is explicitly limited to a non-sensitive demonstration cookie and no longer labels JavaScript session-cookie creation as production-safe.

findingDetails.title: Production CDN-loader guidance omits integrity enforcement and accepts any caller-provided URL
findingDetails.evidence: The production-tested helper assigns a caller-provided URL to script.src and treats version pinning plus error handling as sufficient, without an origin allowlist or integrity attribute.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact searches across all 157 in-scope Markdown files found dynamic script.src examples in five references, one optional crossOrigin assignment, and no integrity assignment; the representative guide was directly reread.

spec_code.note: carried forward from iteration 1; saturated correctness sweep not repeated

checklist_evidence.note: carried forward; BLOCKED approach not retried

noveltyJustification: A security-only pivot found one contradictory session-cookie trust-boundary defect and one third-party script hardening gap; it did not repeat the saturated correctness structural sweep.
edgeCases: Explicit **/*.md globs match 157 files while the configured 163 count includes six non-Markdown assets; excluded hits produced no finding.
SameSite/Secure are valid mitigations, but do not make a JavaScript-accessible session cookie HttpOnly.
Memory trigger timed out; direct evidence fallback used.
ruledOut: approved direct execution of user input

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The configured scope spans multiple security-guidance families across 157 currently matched Markdown files.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Exact high-risk token searches identified the primary trust-boundary, cookie, secret, input-execution, and third-party loader guidance; direct reads supplied evidence and counterevidence.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Positive cookie guidance may cross the client/server trust boundary and omit HttpOnly.

searchActions.result: A GOOD JavaScript session-cookie example and later contradictory BAD example were found in the same file.
SameSite/Secure counterevidence does not supply HttpOnly; later guidance requires server-side Set-Cookie.

searchLedger.rationale: The positively labelled copy-paste pattern contradicts the document's own session-cookie rule.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Production dynamic-script examples may omit origin and byte-integrity controls.

searchActions.queryOrPath: script.src, integrity, and crossOrigin within configured **/*.md scope
searchActions.result: Five dynamic script references, one optional crossOrigin assignment, and no integrity assignment were found.
The helper accepts a URL and labels version pinning/error handling good without documenting a trusted-origin precondition or SRI.

searchLedger.rationale: The omission is actionable supply-chain hardening but not an active exploit in documentation.

searchLedger.invariant: In-scope guidance must not endorse hardcoded credentials.

searchActions.queryOrPath: secret, API key, password, token, credential across configured **/*.md scope
searchActions.result: The representative secret pattern explicitly rejects hardcoding and labels the literal as BAD.

searchLedger.rationale: Matched credential examples were negative demonstrations or operational credential checks.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No positively endorsed hardcoded-secret pattern was found.

searchLedger.invariant: Authorization guidance must require server-side enforcement.

searchActions.result: Both references reject client-side authorization as a security boundary and require server verification.

searchLedger.rationale: The trust boundary is explicit and consistent in the reviewed authorization sections.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No approved client-side-only authorization pattern was found.

searchLedger.invariant: Guidance must not approve direct user-input execution or destructive shell bootstrap patterns.

searchActions.queryOrPath: eval, exec, rm -rf, sudo, chmod 777, curl/wget pipe-to-shell
searchActions.result: Direct-input execution matches were negative examples; no destructive bootstrap pattern matched.

searchLedger.rationale: The scoped guidance rejects direct execution and did not contain the searched destructive command forms.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No positively endorsed instance was found.

title: The CSRF checklist labels a JavaScript-accessible session cookie as good guidance
Production CDN-loader guidance omits integrity enforcement and accepts any caller-provided URL

detail: Compact skeptic/referee retained I2-P1-001 at P1: SameSite/Secure are valid mitigations, but the later server-side HttpOnly rule confirms that positively labelled JavaScript session-cookie creation is unsafe copy-paste guidance; P0 was rejected because no deployed session implementation is in scope.

reason: Direct guidance rejects hardcoding and labels the literal example BAD.

direction: client-side-only authorization and direct user-input execution as approved guidance
reason: Reviewed security references explicitly require server authorization and label direct execution unsafe.
Scope-corrected exact search found no endorsed rm -rf, sudo, chmod 777, or pipe-to-shell pattern.
